back_translation: And we turned and journeyed to the wilderness by the way of the Sea of Reeds, as the Lord spoke to me, and we went around Mount Seir many days.
And the Lord said to me, saying:
You have gone around this mountain long enough; turn yourselves to the north.
And command the people, saying: You are passing through the territory of your brothers, the sons of Esau who dwell in Seir. And they will be afraid of you, so be very careful.
Do not provoke them, for I will not give you any of their land, even as much as the tread of a foot, because I have given Mount Seir to Esau as a possession.
Food you shall buy from them with silver and eat, and also water you shall buy from them with silver and drink.

For the Lord your God has blessed you in all the work of your hand; He has known your walking through this great wilderness. These forty years the Lord your God has been with you; you have not lacked a thing.
And we passed by our brothers, the sons of Esau who dwell in Seir, by the way of the Arabah, from Elath and from Ezion-geber. And we turned and passed by way of the wilderness of Moab.
And the Lord said to me: Do not harass Moab, and do not provoke them in battle, for I will not give you any of his land as a possession, because I have given Ar to the sons of Lot as a possession.
The Emim formerly dwelt in it, a people great and many and tall like the Anakim.
Rephaim they were also reckoned, like the Anakim. And the Moabites call them Emim.

And in Seir, the Horites formerly dwelt, and the sons of Esau dispossessed them and destroyed them from before them and dwelt in their place, just as Israel did to the land of his inheritance that the Lord gave to them.
Now arise and cross over the Brook of Zered for yourselves. And we crossed over the Brook of Zered.

And the days that we walked from Kadesh-barnea until we crossed over the Brook of Zered were thirty-eight years, until the whole generation of the men of war was finished from the midst of the camp, just as the Lord swore to them.
And also the hand of the Lord was against them, to destroy them from the midst of the camp until they were finished.
And it came to pass, when all the men of war had finished dying from the midst of the people,
the Lord spoke to me, saying:
You are crossing today the border of Moab, namely Ar.

And you will approach opposite the sons of Ammon; do not harass them and do not provoke them, for I will not give to you any of the land of the sons of Ammon as a possession, because I have given it to the sons of Lot as a possession.
It too was reckoned as a land of Rephaim; Rephaim dwelt in it formerly, and the Ammonites call them Zamzummim,
a people great and many and tall like the Anakim; but the Lord destroyed them from before them, and they dispossessed them and dwelt in their place,
just as He did for the sons of Esau who dwell in Seir, when He destroyed the Horites from before them, and they dispossessed them and dwelt in their place to this day.
And the Avvim who dwelt in villages as far as Gaza—the Caphtorim, who came out from Caphtor, destroyed them and dwelt in their place.
Arise, journey, and cross over the Brook of Arnon. See, I have given into your hand Sihon king of Heshbon the Amorite and his land. Begin, possess, and provoke him to battle.
This day I will begin to put the dread of you and the fear of you upon the face of the peoples under all the heavens, who will hear your report and tremble and writhe from before you.
And I sent messengers from the wilderness of Kedemoth to Sihon king of Heshbon with words of peace, saying:
Let me pass through your land; in the road, in the road I will walk; I will not turn right or left.
Food for silver you shall sell me, and I will eat; and water for silver you shall give me, and I will drink. Only let me pass through on my feet,
just as the sons of Esau who dwell in Seir and the Moabites who dwell in Ar did for me, until I cross over the Jordan to the land that the Lord our God is giving to us.
But Sihon king of Heshbon was not willing to let us pass through it, for the Lord your God hardened his spirit and strengthened his heart, that He might give him into your hand, as it is this day.
And the Lord said to me: See, I have begun to give before you Sihon and his land. Begin, possess, in order to possess his land.
And Sihon came out to meet us, he and all his people, for battle at Jahaz.
And the Lord our God gave him before us, and we struck him and his sons and all his people.
And we captured all his cities at that time, and we devoted to destruction every city of men, and the women and the children; we left no survivor.
Only the livestock we plundered for ourselves, and the spoil of the cities that we captured.
From Aroer that is on the edge of the Brook of Arnon, and the city that is in the valley, even unto Gilead, there was no city that was too high for us; the Lord our God gave them all before us.
Only to the land of the sons of Ammon you did not come near, all the bank of the Brook of Jabbok and the cities of the hill country and all that the Lord our God commanded.
